generated_oml:
instance VehicleDigitalTwin : DTDFVocab:DigitalTwin [
    base:desc "The digital twin instance responsible for data management, analytics, and control logic."
]

instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "LiDAR sensor emitting raw point cloud data for the Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARDataTransmitted
    base:isContainedIn VehicleDigitalTwin
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Vehicle state metrics sensor providing data such as speed, wheel angle, and acceleration."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateDataTransmitted
    base:isContainedIn VehicleDigitalTwin
]

instance UserCommandSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Virtual sensor representing operator commands received via the UI."
    DTDFVocab:producedData UserCommandDataTransmitted
    base:isContainedIn VehicleDigitalTwin
]

instance LiDARDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
    base:desc "Serialized LiDAR point cloud after data translation."
    base:isContainedIn VehicleDigitalTwin
]

instance VehicleStateDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
    base:desc "Serialized vehicle state metrics after data translation."
    base:isContainedIn VehicleDigitalTwin
]

instance UserCommandDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom UserCommandSensor
    base:desc "Serialized operator command data after translation."
    base:isContainedIn VehicleDigitalTwin
]

instance LiDARData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw LiDAR point cloud data used for clustering and tracking."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringEnabler
    base:isContainedIn VehicleDigitalTwin
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Vehicle state metrics such as speed and steering angle."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringEnabler
    base:isContainedIn VehicleDigitalTwin
]

instance OperatorCommandData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Commands issued by an operator (enable ACC, set speed, emergency brake)."
    DTDFVocab:fromData UserCommandDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RemoteManagementEnabler
    base:isContainedIn VehicleDigitalTwin
]

instance ACCAlgorithmModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Adaptive Cruise Control algorithm computing desired speed based on inter‑vehicle distance."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AccComputationEnabler
    base:isContainedIn VehicleDigitalTwin
]

instance DataMonitoringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler for monitoring vehicle state, object tracks, and path projection."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
    base:isContainedIn VehicleDigitalTwin
]

instance AccComputationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler that runs the ACC algorithm and forwards control commands to the vehicle."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
    base:isContainedIn VehicleDigitalTwin
]

instance RemoteManagementEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler that executes remote commands such as enable/disable ACC or emergency braking."
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
    base:isContainedIn VehicleDigitalTwin
]

instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "User interface service displaying real‑time vehicle data, object tracks, projected path, and allowing report generation."
    DTDFVocab:provides InsightVehicleState, InsightObjectTrack, InsightPathProjection, ActionGenerateReport
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that offloads ACC computation to the Digital Twin and sends the resulting speed command to the vehicle."
    DTDFVocab:provides ActionSendSpeedCommand
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that accepts remote operator commands to manage ACC and vehicle safety functions."
    DTDFVocab:provides ActionEnableACC, ActionSetDesiredSpeed, ActionEmergencyBrake
]

instance InsightVehicleState : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Displays real‑time vehicle state data to the operator."
]

instance InsightObjectTrack : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Shows detected object tracks processed from LiDAR data."
]

instance InsightPathProjection : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visualizes the projected driving path for the vehicle."
]

instance ActionGenerateReport : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Generates a report of the vehicle state data for analysis."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ActionSendSpeedCommand : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Sends the computed desired speed command back to the vehicle."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ActionEnableACC : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Enables Adaptive Cruise Control on the vehicle."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ActionSetDesiredSpeed : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Sets a desired speed for the vehicle via the UI."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ActionEmergencyBrake : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Issues an emergency braking command to the vehicle."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance VehicleTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin environment containing core components."
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Digital twin sends control commands to the physical twin through the Actuation FE. Command types include motor actuation (throttle/brake), steering servo actuation, and other actuator position commands. The document indicates that these commands are communicated via ROS2 messages over the internal network; however, specific serialization formats, authentication mechanisms, conflict resolution strategies, and feedback confirmation procedures are not described."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "All vehicle‑side functional entities rely on a 100 ms clock‑synchronisation accuracy mandated by safety‑critical vehicular standards. ROS 2 nodes within each Functional Entity timestamp their messages using DDS time stamps that are synchronised via a common network time protocol (e.g., IEEE 1588 Precision Time Protocol) to achieve sub‑100 ms jitter. The Digital Twin’s virtual entities maintain a 100 ms temporal granularity, matching the physical twin’s sensor update rates, and the system tolerates computation‑offload latencies up to 100 ms before data propagation back to the vehicle."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The overall orchestration follows ISO 23247 Reference Architecture, mapping each Functional Entity (FE) to an individual ROS 2 package that contains one or more ROS 2 nodes. The User Entity (visualisation, control panel) is implemented as a ROS 2 launch file that starts corresponding nodes; the Digital Twin Entity contains packages for data storage, analytics, and control; the Physical Twin Entity hosts sensor‑feedback, point‑cloud clustering, and tracking packages. Each package communicates over DDS using QoS profiles that guarantee real‑time constraints. The system’s resource management strategy assigns compute‑heavy tasks (e.g., point‑cloud clustering) to high‑performance edge nodes while lightweight node interfaces run on the vehicle’s in‑vehicle network. Coordination among entities is achieved via ROS 2 topics, services, and actions, with a central ROS 2 master node ensuring single‑source time stamps and message routing."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DTS architecture combines a ROS2 internal network (typically using DDS over UDP) for inter‑component communication and a Wi‑Fi interface employing UDP for inter‑entity links between physical, digital, and user entities. The document does not specify concrete bandwidth, latency, reliability, redundancy, or security protocols for either domain."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The system aligns with ISO 23247 – Digital Twin Framework for Manufacturing, Part 2 (Reference Architecture), adopting an entity‑based architecture where observable manufacturing elements are represented by a Digital Twin Entity, data is collected and controlled by a Data Collection and Device Control Entity, and users interact via a User Entity. Functional Entities handle specific tasks such as actuation, data transformation, and user interfacing, though the standard does not prescribe implementation details. OPC Unified Architecture is cited as an example implementation protocol, facilitating interoperability. Compliance thus relies on adherence to ISO 23247 Part 2 specifications and enables interoperability through the established entity relationships and OPC UA compatibility. "
]
